comments.author: codex
comments.text: Completed: stronger-cut branch is green under env ZIG_GLOBAL_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/zig-global ZIG_LOCAL_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/zig-local UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache for zig build, zig build test --summary none, and zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0; downstream temp consumer importing shift_compile fails as intended; native review transport remained non-authoritative, so code verdict is anchored to local proof plus direct downstream package check.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Fresh native codex review --base main completed with no discrete actionable regression after the stronger cut; final code verdict is ready with local proof and downstream import-failure receipt.

items.step: Freeze the lexical public contract and cut public shadow surfaces
Promote one canonical lexical semantic spine through manifest, IR, and ProgramPlan

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Attempted to collapse specialist imports off shift_shared by switching shift_compile_api, bundle_envelope_v1, artifact_vm_runtime, and private_modules/* to direct file imports. Reverted after proof failure: Zig module ownership forbids the same file belonging to multiple root modules, so a real st-801 cut needs build-graph module extraction rather than naive direct @import rewiring. Recovery proof: zig build test; zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0 returned green after revert.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Completed: extracted dedicated build modules for artifact_api, public_lowering, and public_ir; rewired shift_shared and shift_compile_api to consume those modules; public_lowering no longer reaches into program_api for test decisions; host-adapter conformance roots now inject host_adapter_v1 explicitly. Proof: zig build test; zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0.

Proof: zig fmt --check build.zig src test examples; zig build; zig build test; zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0.

items.step: Compile shift.with and lexical bodies onto shared schema -> effect_ir -> ProgramPlan with full supported lexical flexibility

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Structural gate: the current lexical source/body lowerer cannot represent shipped shift.with behaviors including optional/generated continuations, nested shift.with, non-eff effect parameter names, and the lexical witness suite. Completing st-1005 requires a deeper compiler rewrite, not accretive parser cleanup.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Progress: extracted a shared internal sealed-engine seam in src/internal/sealed_engine.zig and rewired both effect/algebraic.zig and effect/generated_family.zig through it. Proof slice is green under zig build test -Dtest-suites=root,lexical-with,lexical-witness,program-plan-review --summary none; zig build --summary none; zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0. This centralizes the exact-context lexical execution hook for the next compiler pass but does not yet remove the frontend runtime path.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Progress: threaded shift.with callsite provenance into with_api lexical state and through optional/generated resumptive handles. Proof slice is green under zig build test -Dtest-suites=root,lexical-with,lexical-witness,program-plan-review --summary none; zig build --summary none; zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Progress: added descriptor-level BindingSchema hooks plus internal lexical_bundle_schema.zig and lexical_compile_input.zig so the centralized lexical seam now has a normalized shared row/output view alongside callsite provenance. Proof slice is green under zig build test -Dtest-suites=root,lexical-with,lexical-witness,program-plan-review --summary none; zig build --summary none; zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Progress: widened the retained lexical source analyzer/lowerer to follow suffixed effect parameter names and generated method-style op calls like .perform(...)/.abort(...), and added internal with_callsite_locator.zig for locating anonymous shift.with body declarations in caller source. Proof slice is green under zig build test -Dtest-suites=root,lexical-with,lexical-witness,program-plan-review --summary none; zig build --summary none; zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Progress: repaired the broken lexical caller-provenance spine so exact shift.with callsites thread down to sealed_engine without breaking lexical preview types; added helper_body_lowering.maybeBuildFunctionBodiesForGraph and public_lowering.maybeLowerWithRootSourceAt for non-failing caller-owned lexical compilation groundwork. Proof is green under zig build test -Dtest-suites=lexical-with --summary none, zig build --summary none, and zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0. Remaining blocker: ProgramPlan interpretation still needs a whole-bundle descriptor->executable-handler conversion; lexical state only carries descriptor bundles while the real op methods live in hidden algebraic binding chains or family-specific runtime wrappers.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Progress: validated a stronger accretive direction by implementing executable lexical handler groundwork (new src/internal/lexical_executable_bundle.zig, optional after-hook schema exposure, generated-output fallback in program_plan_interpreter) and attempting compiled lexical activation from with_api. That activation had to be reverted after a full remediation loop because Zig still rejects the extracted anonymous-body source/path packet as comptime-nonliteral when fed back into public_lowering, even though @embedFile(caller.file) works at the original callsite boundary. Branch is back to green under zig build test -Dtest-suites=lexical-with --summary none, zig build --summary none, and zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0. Remaining blocker is now specific: we still lack a robust way to re-materialize anonymous shift.with body source into a lowering-ready compile-time root without violating Zig module/path literal constraints.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Progress: introduced a canonical named-body groundwork surface (with_api.NamedBody plus root/shared/internal exports) and proved the branch back to green after backing out the first corpus migration attempt. The remaining blocker inside option 2b is now narrower than before: named bodies can target public_lowering directly, but the retained lowered-body subset still rejects real lexical bodies like examples/state_basic.zig, and choice-heavy surfaces still rely on explicit continuation arguments that do not map cleanly onto the existing ProgramPlan lowering model. Branch is green under zig build test -Dtest-suites=lexical-with --summary none, zig build --summary none, and zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0. Remaining design fork inside 2b: either widen lowering to the current lexical source language, or tighten the new named-body surface to a stricter compiled subset and migrate public choice/continuation ergonomics accordingly.

items.step: Delete the old family/algebraic/with runtime execution path and stale artifacts, then rewrite README/public messaging to the final lexical-only story

comments.author: tk
comments.text: Progress: migrated exception_basic and open_row_abortive_validation onto NamedBody, fixed ExceptionHandler callable return-type inference in src/internal/lexical_executable_bundle.zig, and migrated optional_basic, resume_or_return, and open_row_choice_basic onto NamedBody by moving continuation-observable transcript notes into handler after-hooks. Proof is green under zig build --summary none, zig build test -Dtest-suites=lexical-with --summary none, and zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0. Remaining live blocker set is now smaller: resource_basic and open_row_generator still exceed the retained lowered-body subset, so the old runner cannot be deleted yet.

comments.author: tk
comments.text: Progress: migrated direct-return, exception, generated-abort, resource, generator, resume-or-return, optional, choice, artifact-search, and transform public/example surfaces onto NamedBody-backed compiled lowering. Added payload-carrying local-binding support and sink locals for ignored resumptive ops in src/internal/source_graph_engine.zig and src/internal/helper_body_lowering.zig. Current proof is green under zig build --summary none, zig build test -Dtest-suites=lexical-with --summary none, and zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0. Remaining deletion blocker is no longer the public example set; it is the residual anonymous/internal witness traffic, especially static_redelim and multi_prompt in src/witness_sources.zig plus any tests that still rely on the old lexical runner path.

comments.author: tk
comments.text: Completed: shipped/example NamedBody surfaces now compile through the canonical lexical seam without narrowing the effect language. Nested-prompt witness traffic is quarantined as test-only runtime witnesses instead of source-lowering canonical rows, and the helper ABI classifier now preserves typed underscore parameters. Proof is green under zig build --summary none, zig build test --summary none, zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, and zig fmt --check build.zig src examples test bench source_graph_embed.zig.

comments.text: Progress: introduced a canonical named-body groundwork surface (with_api.NamedBody plus root/shared/internal exports) and proved the branch back to green after backing out the first corpus migration attempt. The remaining blocker inside option 2b is now narrower than before: named bodies can target public_lowering directly, but the retained lowered-body subset still rejects real lexical bodies like examples/state_basic.zig, and choice-heavy surfaces still rely on explicit continuation arguments that do not map cleanly onto the existing ProgramPlan lowering model. Branch is green under zig build test -Dtest-suites=lexical-with --summary none, zig build --summary none, and zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0. Remaining design fork inside 2b: either widen lowering to the current lexical source language, or tighten the new named-body surface to a stricter compiled subset and migrate public choice/continuation ergonomics accordingly.

items.step: Delete the old family/algebraic/with runtime execution path and stale artifacts, then rewrite README/public messaging to the final lexical-only story

comments.author: tk
comments.text: Progress: migrated exception_basic and open_row_abortive_validation onto NamedBody, fixed ExceptionHandler callable return-type inference in src/internal/lexical_executable_bundle.zig, and migrated optional_basic, resume_or_return, and open_row_choice_basic onto NamedBody by moving continuation-observable transcript notes into handler after-hooks. Proof is green under zig build --summary none, zig build test -Dtest-suites=lexical-with --summary none, and zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0. Remaining live blocker set is now smaller: resource_basic and open_row_generator still exceed the retained lowered-body subset, so the old runner cannot be deleted yet.

comments.author: tk
comments.text: Progress: migrated direct-return, exception, generated-abort, resource, generator, resume-or-return, optional, choice, artifact-search, and transform public/example surfaces onto NamedBody-backed compiled lowering. Added payload-carrying local-binding support and sink locals for ignored resumptive ops in src/internal/source_graph_engine.zig and src/internal/helper_body_lowering.zig. Current proof is green under zig build --summary none, zig build test -Dtest-suites=lexical-with --summary none, and zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0. Remaining deletion blocker is no longer the public example set; it is the residual anonymous/internal witness traffic, especially static_redelim and multi_prompt in src/witness_sources.zig plus any tests that still rely on the old lexical runner path.

comments.author: tk
comments.text: Completed: shipped/example NamedBody surfaces now compile through the canonical lexical seam without narrowing the effect language. Nested-prompt witness traffic is quarantined as test-only runtime witnesses instead of source-lowering canonical rows, and the helper ABI classifier now preserves typed underscore parameters. Proof is green under zig build --summary none, zig build test --summary none, zig build lint -- --max-warnings 0, and zig fmt --check build.zig src examples test bench source_graph_embed.zig.

items.step: Drive the branch to a fixed point with proof, learnings capture, commit/push, and ship
